claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is determined on a per-child basis, so I'll need information about each child you're inquiring about.

**First question: Do you have a child or children you're responsible for? If yes, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to check if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child I'm asking about.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! That's helpful. Now I need to ask about your child's age, as this is a key factor in eligibility.

**How old is your child?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child Alex is 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 17 years old, they fall into the 16-19 age category, which means there are additional requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in approved education or training, or have they recently left education or training?**

(For context: For 16-19 year olds to qualify, they generally need to be in approved education or training, or have recently left and be within a certain extension period.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex recently left education. They've signed up with the careers service to figure out what they want to do next.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Since Alex recently left education, there may be an extension period during which you can still claim Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news - registering with the careers service is one of the qualifying services for the extension period. Now I need to know:

**How long ago did Alex leave education? (Please tell me approximately how many weeks ago)**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been a couple of months ago now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. A couple of months would be approximately 8-9 weeks, which is within the 20-week extension period. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at all right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Let me continue checking the eligibility criteria.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex doing an apprenticeship of any kind?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't doing an apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Now I need to check your residency status. **Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Do you live with Alex, or do you contribute to their upkeep (such as paying for their care, food, clothes, or maintenance)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (such as another parent or guardian)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations:**
- In hospital
- In local authority care
- Living with foster parents

**Or is Alex living normally at home with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living normally at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why Alex qualifies:
- Alex is 17 years old and recently left education (approximately 8-9 weeks ago)
- Alex registered with the careers service, which qualifies for the 20-week extension period for 16-19 year olds who have left education
- Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week
- Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right
- Alex is not doing an apprenticeship
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- You live with Alex and are responsible for them
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance

**Important things to note:**

1. **Time-sensitive:** The 20-week extension period started when Alex left education. Since it's been about 8-9 weeks, you have approximately 11-12 weeks remaining in this extension period, or until Alex turns 18, whichever comes first.

2. **Next steps to claim:**
   - Apply online or by post using form CH2
   - Provide proof of identity for yourself and Alex
   - Provide Alex's birth certificate

3. **High Income Child Benefit Charge:** If you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through the tax system.

Would you like any clarification about the eligibility or the claims process?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's great, thank you so much for explaining everything! That's really helpful to know about the time limit on the extension period - I'll make sure to apply right away. I appreciate all the information!
